prompt: The task list is showing duplicate tasks after drag-and-drop reorder

turns.content:
<task>
The task list is showing duplicate tasks after drag-and-drop reorder. Users report seeing the same task twice after moving it.
</task>

arguments.reasoning: Found the bug. On line 10-11, the code copies the array and inserts the dragged item at the drop index, but never removes it from its original position. The splice on line 11 only inserts — it doesn't remove. The fix is to first remove the item from dragIndex, then insert at dropIndex. Classic drag-and-drop mistake.

arguments.message: Found the bug in src/hooks/useTaskList.ts. The handleReorder function inserts the dragged item at the drop position but never removes it from its original position. Line 11 only does `splice(dropIndex, 0, item)` (insert) without first doing `splice(dragIndex, 1)` (remove). This causes the duplicate.

arguments.summary: Identified drag-and-drop duplication bug: handleReorder inserts item at new position without removing from old position.

next_steps.description: Fix handleReorder in useTaskList.ts to remove item from dragIndex before inserting at dropIndex
Verify the fix handles edge case where dragIndex > dropIndex

arguments.title: Drag-and-drop reorder pattern
arguments.content: Correct pattern: splice(dragIndex, 1) to remove, then splice(dropIndex, 0, item) to insert. Must remove first. Also add early return for dragIndex === dropIndex.

arguments.summary: Fixed drag-and-drop duplication by removing the item from its original index before inserting at the new position. Added early return for same-index case. TypeScript compiles cleanly.
arguments.final_answer: Fixed the duplicate task bug in useTaskList.ts. The reorder function was only inserting the dragged item without removing it from the original position. Now uses splice(dragIndex, 1) to remove first, then splice(dropIndex, 0, item) to insert.
